title: Hylo LSP project manifest
description: A build-system-agnostic description of a Hylo module graph. A build system (CMake, Bazel, a future SPM-like tool, or `hc` itself) emits this file as `hylo-project.json` so the Hylo language server can group a workspace's files into modules and wire their imports without knowing anything about the build system. It is the equivalent of rust-analyzer's `rust-project.json`.

$schema.description: Optional pointer to this JSON Schema, so an editor's JSON language server can offer completion and validation.

schemaVersion.description: The manifest schema version. Increment on a breaking change to this format so a server can reject a manifest it cannot interpret. Current version: 1.

stdlibRoot.description: The standard library's root source directory, if the build system knows it. When a module named "Hylo" *is* present in `modules`, that module is authoritative and this field is redundant.

target.description: The compilation target triple (e.g. "x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"), if specified.

modules.description: The modules in the workspace. Each entry is the Hylo slice of one or more build targets.

module.description: One Hylo module: a set of source files compiled and type-checked together, plus the modules it imports. A module whose `name` is "Hylo" is the standard library: the server builds it as the base that every other module implicitly depends on.

name.description: The module's name, exactly as written in `import` statements. The name "Hylo" is reserved for the standard library. Because two targets can define modules of the same name (e.g. two executables' `Main`), `name` alone is not a workspace-unique key; `(originTarget, name)` is.

originTarget.description: The build target that contributed these sources, if any. For diagnostics and traceability only, and to disambiguate two modules that share a `name`.

archive.description: Path to a published `.hylomodule` archive of this module's compiled interface, if the build produced one. Optional. When present and fresh, a server *may* load the interface from here instead of recompiling from `sources`/`sourceRoot`; when absent, the server compiles the module from source (the reliable default).

imports.description: The names of the modules this one imports (direct edges only). The server walks the transitive closure itself; the standard library need not be listed (it is an implicit dependency of every module).

sources.description: Explicit list of this module's `.hylo` source files, as absolute paths. Use for generated sources or files scattered outside a single directory. Combined by union with any files discovered under `sourceRoot`. At least one of `sources` or `sourceRoot` must be present.

sourceRoot.description: A single directory whose `.hylo` files (searched recursively) all belong to this module. Relative paths are resolved against the directory containing this manifest; absolute paths are used as-is. This is the common form — the standard library, for example, is declared as `{ "name": "Hylo", "sourceRoot": "Sources" }`. Combined by union with any explicit `sources`. At least one of `sources` or `sourceRoot` must be present.
